Sweden is traditionally divided into 25 provinces (landskap) grouped into three major regions: Götaland in the south, Svealand in the central area, and Norrland in the north. Götaland includes provinces like Scania (Skåne), known for fertile plains and beech forests; Småland, famous for its lakes, forests, and glassblowing industry; and Gotland, a limestone island in the Baltic with unique geological formations and medieval architecture. Svealand contains Uppland with Uppsala and the northern parts of Stockholm; Dalarna, known for traditional Swedish culture, red Dala horses, and scenic mountains; and Värmland, a lake-rich forest region associated with Swedish author Selma Lagerlöf. Norrland comprises the northern half of the country, including Lapland (Lappland) with vast wilderness, the Kebnekaise mountain, and the Abisko National Park known for aurora borealis viewing; Norrbotten with the Torne River valley and Sami reindeer herding culture; and Jämtland, a popular skiing and outdoor recreation destination.
